context.author: Sabrina Lin
context.note: Reflection after completing the first structured spatial observation and initializing the dataset repository.

schema_thinking.description: Schema thinking is the cognitive process of translating complex human experiences into a structured, repeatable, and protocol-governed data model.
schema_thinking.core_principle: Define a stable structure early, then refine and collect observations according to the same protocol over time.
workflow: review and refine the schema through repeated application

example_application.purpose: Transform spatial experience into a structured dataset that can be compared across locations and interpreted by both humans and AI.

observation.definition: The ability to perceive and articulate subtle spatial, sensory, and atmospheric qualities in a physical environment.
observation.role_in_dataset: Provides the raw experiential input for each observation record.

schema_thinking.definition: The ability to design structured data models that translate qualitative experiences into measurable and comparable dimensions.
schema_thinking.role_in_dataset: Defines the structure of the SDIndex framework and the JSON schema used for the dataset.

consistency.definition: The discipline to apply the same observation protocol and schema across all sampling sites.
consistency.role_in_dataset: Ensures that observations remain comparable and that the dataset maintains methodological integrity over time.

research_implication.summary: The SDIndex framework represents a form of human sensory data modeling, where spatial perception is systematically captured through observation, structured schema design, and consistent field protocols.
research_implication.future_significance: If sustained over a large number of observations, this approach can generate a unique human sensory dataset for spatial environments.
